codebase_summary:
This codebase implements an enterprise GenAI Evaluation Framework and Publishing Ethics Compliance Platform for Elsevier. The system addresses two core business needs: (1) It provides a structured way to manage, evaluate, and safely promote AI-powered applications — specifically Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) applications that answer questions using company FAQ documents. It enforces a disciplined prompt version lifecycle where new prompt configurations must be validated against golden test suites before they can be activated in production, preventing untested AI behavior from reaching end users. (2) It implements an Automated Reasoning (AR) compliance system that validates AI-generated publishing ethics assessments against Elsevier's formal publishing policies — covering authorship integrity, conflict of interest disclosure, simultaneous submission prohibition, clinical trial registration, plagiarism, and image manipulation.

The Eval Framework serves platform engineering and AI operations teams who need to ensure quality and safety of GenAI applications. It provides a golden dataset management system for maintaining curated test cases, a prompt registry for versioning prompt configurations, LLM-as-judge scoring for automated quality assessment, and Bedrock Guardrails integration for content safety, PII detection, and contextual grounding checks. The AR Ethics Advisor serves publishing compliance officers who need to verify that manuscript submissions comply with Elsevier's ethics policies. Together, these systems form a governance layer ensuring that AI outputs are accurate, safe, policy-compliant, and traceable.

The platform is designed around a promote-through-validation philosophy: no AI configuration change goes live without passing automated evaluation. Test suites are snapshotted for reproducibility, prompt versions carry full audit trails, and compliance assessments are verified through formal automated reasoning rather than relying solely on LLM judgment. This creates a defensible, auditable AI governance framework suitable for regulated publishing environments.

business_domains.description: The core AI-powered question-answering service that retrieves relevant FAQ documents and generates natural-language answers using configurable prompts. This is the production application that end users interact with.
Infrastructure provisioning and deployment pipeline for the RAG application, including IAM roles, Lambda functions, S3 document storage, and API Gateway endpoints.

Manages the lifecycle of prompt configurations — creation, validation, activation, and version tracking. Enforces the policy that prompts must be validated before activation, supporting safe iteration on AI behavior.
Centralized registry of all GenAI applications under governance, tracking their type, endpoints, model providers, ownership, and operational status.

Manages curated test suites used for evaluating AI application quality. Supports test case CRUD, categorization by criticality and category, snapshotting for reproducible evaluation runs, and soft-delete archival to preserve audit history.

Automated quality and safety evaluation of AI responses. Includes LLM-as-judge scoring across multiple dimensions and Bedrock Guardrails checks for content safety, PII detection, and contextual grounding (RAG faithfulness).

Automated Reasoning-based compliance validation for publishing ethics. Generates structured compliance assessments of manuscript submissions and verifies them against formal policy rules covering authorship, disclosure, simultaneous submission, clinical trials, plagiarism, and data integrity.

End-to-end management of Automated Reasoning policies — creating policies, ingesting ethics documents to extract formal rules, reviewing fidelity scores, and attaching policies to guardrails for runtime enforcement.
Provisioning of shared infrastructure resources — S3 buckets, DynamoDB tables, guardrails — that underpin the evaluation framework and application registry.

Streamlit-based web UI enabling platform users to browse registered apps, manage test suites, run evaluations, configure prompts, and interact with the AR Ethics Advisor — all through a unified navigation experience.
Utility for generating architecture presentation slides, used for documentation and stakeholder communication.

function_classifications.business_summary: Provisions the security identity (IAM role) that allows the RAG application to access AI models, document storage, and prompt configuration.
Publishes the company FAQ knowledge base to cloud storage so the RAG application can retrieve it at runtime to answer customer questions.
Packages the RAG application code into a deployable artifact.
Deploys or updates the RAG application as a serverless function, making the question-answering service available for use.
Creates the public API endpoint through which users and evaluation systems can send questions to the RAG application.
Orchestrates the complete deployment of the RAG application: provisions security, uploads knowledge base, deploys the service, and exposes the API endpoint.

Loads the currently active prompt configuration from the registry. If no active prompt is found at any step (no pointer, no version record, no active version), it falls back to safe defaults — ensuring the application always has a working configuration.
Defines the fallback prompt configuration used when no active prompt version exists in the registry, ensuring the RAG app can always serve responses.
Retrieves the company FAQ knowledge base from cloud storage for use in answering queries.

Performs keyword-based document retrieval: scores each FAQ document by how many query terms appear in it, ranks by relevance, and returns the top-K most relevant documents. Only documents with at least one matching term are returned.

Generates a natural-language answer by sending the user's query and retrieved context documents to the AI model (Bedrock Claude), using the active prompt configuration's system prompt, context template, temperature, and token limits.

The main RAG application entry point. Validates the incoming query, determines the prompt configuration (active version or test override), retrieves relevant documents, generates an answer, and returns the response with metadata about which documents were used and which prompt version was applied.

Executes all publishing ethics compliance scenarios end-to-end: generates an LLM assessment for each scenario, runs AR compliance checks, compares actual verdicts against expected outcomes, and produces a summary scorecard showing pass/fail rates.
Loads the AR policy identifier from local configuration, failing fast if the policy hasn't been created yet.

Reviews an AR policy's fidelity report — displays coverage and accuracy scores, lists extracted variables, custom types, rules, and per-rule fidelity details. Warns when coverage or accuracy scores fall below the 0.7 threshold.
Orchestrates the policy review step: loads the policy ARN and displays the full fidelity report.
Checks whether an AR policy with the expected name already exists to prevent duplicate creation.

Creates a new Automated Reasoning policy for validating publishing ethics compliance, or returns the existing one if already created. The policy is described as covering authorship, disclosure, simultaneous submission, and related ethics rules.
Persists the AR policy identifier to local configuration for use by subsequent pipeline steps.
Orchestrates Step 1 of the AR policy setup: creates the policy and saves its identifier.
Loads the AR policy identifier from local configuration for guardrail attachment.
Checks whether the AR guardrail already exists to prevent duplicate creation.
Creates a Bedrock guardrail that enforces the AR publishing ethics policy at runtime, enabling automated compliance verification of LLM outputs.
Persists the guardrail identifier and version to local configuration for use by compliance checking components.
Orchestrates Step 4 of the AR policy setup: attaches the AR policy to a guardrail and saves the guardrail ID.
Loads the AR policy identifier from local configuration for the build step.
Retrieves the latest policy build workflow ID, used for resuming an in-progress build.

Initiates the AR policy build by uploading the Elsevier publishing ethics document (TXT or PDF) to extract formal rules. Handles the case where a build is already in progress by resuming the existing workflow.
Monitors the policy build workflow, waiting until rule extraction completes or fails.
Orchestrates Step 2 of the AR policy setup: starts the document-to-rules build process and reports the number of extracted variables, rules, and types.
Initializes and caches the AWS Bedrock client for the AR Ethics Advisor UI.
Sends a publishing ethics question to the Claude AI model and returns its compliance assessment response.

Applies the AR guardrail to an LLM-generated compliance assessment, classifying findings as invalid (failed rules with reasons), valid (passed rules), or ambiguous. Returns a PASS/FAIL verdict based on whether any rules were violated.
Maps an AR rule identifier to its human-readable short label for display purposes.

Generates a structured publishing ethics compliance assessment using Claude AI. The assessment covers 11 specific compliance dimensions: simultaneous submission, authorship contributions, authorship changes, conflict of interest existence and declaration, external funding existence and declaration, clinical trial status and registration, plagiarism, and image manipulation.

Runs formal AR guardrail verification on an LLM compliance assessment. Categorizes findings into failed rules (with contradiction reasons), passed rules, ambiguous findings, and unresolvable findings. Determines the final verdict: FAIL if any rules are violated, UNABLE_TO_DETERMINE if only unresolvable findings exist, or PASS if all rules are satisfied.

Runs Bedrock Guardrails evaluation on an AI response, checking content safety, PII exposure, and contextual grounding (whether the answer is faithful to the retrieved context). For RAG applications, context is passed as a grounding source to verify factual faithfulness.

Parses guardrail assessment results into structured check results across three categories: content policy violations (hate, violence, etc.), PII detections (email, phone, SSN, credit cards), and contextual grounding scores (grounding and relevance vs. threshold).

Evaluates an AI response's quality using an LLM-as-judge approach. Sends the query, expected output, retrieved context, and actual answer to a judge model that scores across multiple dimensions on a 1-5 scale. Uses temperature 0.0 for deterministic scoring. Context is capped at 3000 characters.

Creates a new golden dataset test case with full metadata: input query, expected output, ground truth context, evaluation criteria, category, criticality level, tags, and audit information (creator, timestamp, version).
Updates an existing test case's fields while automatically recording the updater, timestamp, and change reason for audit purposes.
Soft-deletes a test case by marking it as archived rather than physically deleting it, preserving audit history and preventing data loss.
Retrieves a single test case by app name and test case ID.
Lists test cases for an application with filtering by status (active/archived), criticality level, and category. Defaults to showing only active test cases.

Creates a point-in-time snapshot of the active test suite to S3, producing an immutable record for reproducible evaluation runs. Snapshots are what eval runs use — not the live DynamoDB state — ensuring evaluation consistency even if test cases are modified later.
Lists all historical test suite snapshots for an application, ordered newest first.
Loads test cases from a previously created S3 snapshot for use in an evaluation run.
Bulk uploads a set of test cases to both DynamoDB (for management) and S3 (for legacy compatibility), used during initial bootstrap of the evaluation framework.
Calculates the next sequential version number for an application's prompt versions (v1, v2, v3...), ensuring monotonically increasing version identifiers.

Creates a new prompt version with the given system prompt, context template, temperature, max tokens, and model ID. The version is created in an un-validated, inactive state — it cannot be activated until it passes evaluation.

Activates a prompt version, making it the live configuration used by the application. Enforces the critical business rule that only validated prompt versions can be activated. Deactivates the previous active version and updates the active pointer atomically.
Records that a prompt version has passed evaluation, storing the validation timestamp and score. This is a prerequisite for activation.
Lists all prompt versions for an application, ordered newest first, excluding the active pointer record.
Retrieves a specific prompt version by app name and version identifier.
Retrieves the currently active prompt version by following the active pointer in the registry.
Seeds the initial default prompt version for a new application if no versions exist. Auto-validates and activates it with a baseline score of 0.0, bootstrapping the prompt lifecycle.
Registers a new GenAI application in the governance framework, recording its type (llm/rag/agentic), endpoint, model provider, model ID, owner, and environment.
Lists all registered GenAI applications with their type, model provider, and status.
Retrieves a single registered application by name.
Marks an application as inactive (soft deactivation), removing it from active governance without deleting its records.

Orchestrates the complete bootstrap of the GenAI Eval Framework: creates infrastructure, deploys the RAG app, registers it in the app registry, seeds a default prompt version, and uploads the golden test suite.

Creates the evaluation framework's Bedrock Guardrail with content safety filters (hate, violence, sexual, misconduct, prompt attacks), PII detection (email, phone, SSN, credit cards), and contextual grounding checks (grounding threshold 0.7, relevance threshold 0.7) for RAG faithfulness verification.
Persists the guardrail identifier and version to local configuration for use by the evaluation engine.
Orchestrates guardrail creation and configuration persistence.
Creates and version-enables the S3 bucket used to store golden datasets and test suite snapshots.
Creates the DynamoDB table for the application registry.
Creates the DynamoDB table for test case metadata, keyed by app name and test case ID.
Creates the DynamoDB table for the prompt registry, keyed by app name and version.
Orchestrates creation of all infrastructure resources: S3 bucket, app registry table, test case table, and prompt registry table.
Utility function for adding formatted boxes with text to presentation slides.
Utility function for populating table rows with formatted text in presentation slides.
Initializes and caches AWS DynamoDB and S3 clients for the Streamlit UI.
Loads all active registered applications for display in the UI dashboard.
Loads test cases from S3 for a specific app and version, enabling the UI to display and run evaluations.
Loads FAQ documents from S3 for display as context in the evaluation UI.
Invokes a registered GenAI application's endpoint with a query and measures response latency, returning success/failure status and response data.
Invokes a registered application with a prompt override, enabling testing of new prompt versions without activating them in production. This supports the validate-before-promote workflow.
Navigates the UI to the evaluation page for a selected application.
Navigates the UI to the configuration page for a selected application.
Navigates the UI back to the app registry home page.
Renders the top navigation bar allowing users to switch between the Eval Framework and AR Ethics Advisor sections.
Sends a publishing ethics question to Claude AI for compliance assessment via the main UI's AR section.
Applies AR guardrail compliance checking from the main UI, classifying findings as invalid, valid, or ambiguous and returning a PASS/FAIL verdict.

business_rules.given: A prompt version exists for an application
business_rules.when: An operator attempts to activate the prompt version
business_rules.then: The activation MUST be rejected unless the version has been marked as validated; if validated, the version becomes active, the previous active version is deactivated, and the active pointer is updated
business_rules.business_impact: If this rule is violated, untested or underperforming prompt configurations could reach production, causing degraded answer quality, hallucinations, or policy violations in customer-facing AI responses.

business_rules.rule_name: Query Field Required for RAG Requests
business_rules.given: A request is received by the RAG application
business_rules.when: The request body does not contain a 'query' field or it is empty
business_rules.then: The application MUST return HTTP 400 with error message 'Missing query field' and MUST NOT invoke the AI model
business_rules.business_impact: Without this validation, empty queries would waste AI model invocations and return nonsensical responses, degrading user experience and incurring unnecessary costs.

business_rules.given: A request is received by the RAG application with a '_prompt_override' field
business_rules.when: The eval framework sends a request to test a candidate prompt version
business_rules.then: The application MUST use the override prompt configuration instead of the active production prompt, enabling safe testing without activation
business_rules.business_impact: Without prompt override capability, every prompt change would need to be activated in production before it can be evaluated, violating the validate-before-promote principle.

business_rules.rule_name: Fallback to Default Prompt on Registry Failure
business_rules.given: The RAG application attempts to load the active prompt configuration
business_rules.when: The prompt registry is unavailable, has no active pointer, or the referenced version does not exist
business_rules.then: The application MUST fall back to the hardcoded default prompt configuration and continue serving responses rather than failing
business_rules.business_impact: Without graceful fallback, a registry outage or misconfiguration would cause complete application downtime instead of degraded but functional service.

business_rules.given: A user query is being processed by the RAG application
business_rules.when: The retrieval system scores FAQ documents against the query
business_rules.then: Only documents with a relevance score greater than zero (at least one matching term) MUST be returned; documents with no term overlap are excluded regardless of the top-K limit
business_rules.business_impact: Including irrelevant documents in the context would cause the AI model to generate answers based on unrelated information, leading to inaccurate or confusing responses.

business_rules.given: A user query is being processed by the RAG application
business_rules.when: Multiple FAQ documents score above zero relevance
business_rules.then: At most 3 (top_k) documents MUST be returned, ranked by descending relevance score
business_rules.business_impact: Returning too many documents would exceed AI model context limits or dilute the relevant information, degrading answer quality and increasing costs.

business_rules.given: A test case exists in the golden dataset
business_rules.when: An operator requests to remove the test case
business_rules.then: The test case MUST be marked as 'archived' (soft delete) and MUST NEVER be physically deleted, preserving full audit history
business_rules.business_impact: Hard deletion of test cases would destroy audit trails, making it impossible to understand historical evaluation results or reproduce past evaluation runs.

business_rules.rule_name: Default Filter to Active Test Cases
business_rules.given: An operator or system queries test cases for an application
business_rules.when: No explicit status filter is specified
business_rules.then: Only test cases with status 'active' MUST be returned; archived cases are excluded by default
business_rules.business_impact: Including archived test cases in evaluation runs would produce inaccurate quality metrics based on outdated or invalidated test expectations.

business_rules.given: An evaluation run needs to be executed against a test suite
business_rules.when: The evaluation system requests test cases
business_rules.then: A point-in-time snapshot MUST be created from active test cases and stored immutably in S3; the evaluation run MUST use the snapshot, NOT the live DynamoDB state
business_rules.business_impact: Without snapshots, test cases modified during an evaluation run would produce inconsistent results, making evaluation outcomes unreproducible and unreliable.

business_rules.given: A snapshot creation is requested for an application
business_rules.when: The application has no active test cases
business_rules.then: The snapshot creation MUST raise a ValueError and MUST NOT create an empty snapshot
business_rules.business_impact: Empty snapshots would cause evaluation runs to pass vacuously with no test cases executed, creating a false sense of quality assurance.

business_rules.given: A new prompt version is being created for an application
business_rules.when: The system calculates the next version identifier
business_rules.then: The version MUST be assigned the next sequential number (v1, v2, v3...) based on the highest existing version number
business_rules.business_impact: Non-sequential version numbers would create confusion in the audit trail and make it difficult to understand the chronological order of prompt changes.

business_rules.rule_name: New Prompt Versions Created Inactive and Unvalidated
business_rules.given: A new prompt version is being created
business_rules.when: The create_version function is called
business_rules.then: The new version MUST have validated=False and is_active=False, ensuring it cannot serve production traffic until explicitly validated and activated
business_rules.business_impact: If new versions were created in an active or validated state, untested prompts could immediately affect production responses.

business_rules.given: An application has an active prompt version
business_rules.when: A different version is activated
business_rules.then: The previously active version MUST be deactivated (is_active=False) and the active pointer MUST be updated to point to the new version, ensuring exactly one active version at any time
business_rules.business_impact: Multiple active versions would cause non-deterministic application behavior where different requests could be served by different prompt configurations.

business_rules.given: An application has no prompt versions and is being bootstrapped
business_rules.when: The seed_default_version function is called
business_rules.then: A default v1 MUST be created, auto-validated with score 0.0, and immediately activated so the application can start serving
business_rules.business_impact: Without auto-seeding, new applications would have no active prompt and could not serve any responses until a full validation cycle completes.

business_rules.given: An LLM-generated compliance assessment has been checked against the AR guardrail
business_rules.when: The system determines the final compliance verdict
business_rules.then: The verdict MUST be FAIL if any rules are violated (contradicted), UNABLE_TO_DETERMINE if only unresolvable findings exist and no rules passed, or PASS if rules are satisfied with no violations
business_rules.business_impact: Incorrect verdict determination could allow non-compliant manuscripts to be published or block compliant submissions, causing either ethical violations or unnecessary publication delays.

business_rules.rule_name: AR Guardrail ID Required for Compliance Check
business_rules.given: A compliance check is requested
business_rules.when: The AR_GUARDRAIL_ID is not configured
business_rules.then: The system MUST raise a RuntimeError indicating that the guardrail setup step has not been completed, preventing silent failures
business_rules.business_impact: Without this check, compliance verification would silently fail, allowing manuscripts through without any ethics review.

business_rules.rule_name: Compliance Assessment Must Cover All 11 Ethics Dimensions
business_rules.given: A manuscript submission description is provided for compliance assessment
business_rules.when: The LLM generates a compliance assessment
business_rules.then: The assessment MUST address all 11 compliance dimensions: simultaneous submission, authorship contributions, authorship changes after acceptance, conflict of interest existence, conflict of interest declaration, external funding existence, funding declaration, clinical trial status, trial registration, plagiarism, and image manipulation
business_rules.business_impact: Missing any compliance dimension could allow specific types of ethics violations to go undetected, undermining the integrity of the compliance process.

business_rules.rule_name: Demo Verdict Matching Tolerance for Expected Failures
business_rules.given: A compliance demo scenario has an expected verdict of FAIL
business_rules.when: The AR system returns a verdict
business_rules.then: Both FAIL and UNABLE_TO_DETERMINE verdicts MUST be considered a match for expected FAIL scenarios, providing tolerance for AR ambiguity
business_rules.business_impact: Without this tolerance, ambiguous-but-suspicious findings would be treated as test failures, misrepresenting the system's ability to catch non-compliant submissions.

business_rules.given: An AR policy has been built from an ethics document
business_rules.when: The fidelity report's coverage or accuracy score is below 0.7
business_rules.then: The system MUST display a WARNING indicating that the policy may be insufficient and suggesting corrective actions (add more detail to the PDF for low coverage, review rule justifications for low accuracy)
business_rules.business_impact: Using a low-fidelity policy without warning could lead to false compliance verdicts — either failing compliant submissions or passing non-compliant ones.

business_rules.given: An AR policy or guardrail with the expected name already exists
business_rules.when: The creation script is run again
business_rules.then: The system MUST return the existing resource's identifier without creating a duplicate, ensuring idempotent and safe re-runs
business_rules.business_impact: Duplicate policy/guardrail creation would cause confusion about which resources are active and could lead to inconsistent compliance checking.

business_rules.given: The evaluation framework guardrail is being configured
business_rules.when: Content safety filters are applied
business_rules.then: HATE, VIOLENCE, MISCONDUCT, and SEXUAL content MUST be filtered at HIGH strength; INSULTS at MEDIUM strength; PROMPT_ATTACK at HIGH for input only (NONE for output)
business_rules.business_impact: Incorrect filter strength could either block legitimate content (too strict) or allow harmful content through to users (too lenient).

business_rules.given: An AI response is being checked for PII
business_rules.when: The guardrail detects personal information
business_rules.then: Email, Phone, and Name MUST be ANONYMIZED in output; US Social Security Numbers and Credit/Debit Card Numbers MUST be BLOCKED entirely
business_rules.business_impact: Exposing SSN or credit card numbers in AI responses would violate data privacy regulations and could lead to identity theft or financial fraud.

business_rules.given: A RAG application response is being evaluated
business_rules.when: Contextual grounding is assessed
business_rules.then: Both GROUNDING (faithfulness to retrieved context) and RELEVANCE (relevance to query) MUST meet a minimum threshold of 0.7; scores below this threshold indicate the response is not adequately grounded or relevant
business_rules.business_impact: Low grounding thresholds would allow hallucinated or irrelevant responses to pass evaluation, undermining the faithfulness guarantee that RAG is supposed to provide.

business_rules.rule_name: LLM Judge Temperature Must Be Zero
business_rules.given: An LLM-as-judge evaluation is being executed
business_rules.when: The judge model is invoked
business_rules.then: The temperature MUST be set to 0.0 for deterministic, reproducible scoring
business_rules.business_impact: Non-zero temperature would introduce randomness into evaluation scores, making quality metrics non-reproducible and unreliable for promote/no-promote decisions.

business_rules.rule_name: Context Length Cap for Judge Evaluation
business_rules.given: An LLM-as-judge evaluation is processing retrieved context
business_rules.when: The context exceeds 3000 characters
business_rules.then: The context MUST be truncated to 3000 characters to prevent exceeding model input limits
business_rules.business_impact: Exceeding context limits would cause judge invocations to fail, preventing evaluation from completing.

business_rules.given: A guardrail evaluation is requested
business_rules.when: No guardrail ID is configured
business_rules.then: The system MUST return passed=True with action='NO_GUARDRAIL' and an explanatory error message, allowing evaluation to continue without guardrail checks rather than failing entirely
business_rules.business_impact: Hard failure when no guardrail is configured would prevent any evaluation from running during initial setup or in environments where guardrails are not yet provisioned.

business_rules.rule_name: S3 Bucket Versioning for Golden Datasets
business_rules.given: The golden dataset S3 bucket is created
business_rules.when: Bucket configuration is applied
business_rules.then: Versioning MUST be enabled on the bucket to ensure all historical versions of test suite data are preserved
business_rules.business_impact: Without versioning, overwritten test data cannot be recovered, making it impossible to reproduce historical evaluation runs or audit data changes.

business_rules.given: An application is registered in the governance framework
business_rules.when: An operator deactivates the application
business_rules.then: The application status MUST be set to 'inactive' (soft deactivation) and the record MUST be preserved, not physically deleted
business_rules.business_impact: Hard deletion of application records would destroy the governance history and make it impossible to audit past evaluation results for that application.

business_rules.given: The UI dashboard loads the list of applications
business_rules.when: Applications are fetched from the registry
business_rules.then: Only applications with status 'active' MUST be displayed, hiding deactivated applications from the operational view
business_rules.business_impact: Showing deactivated apps would confuse operators and potentially lead to running evaluations against deprecated applications.

business_rules.given: The evaluation system invokes a registered application
business_rules.when: The application does not respond within 30 seconds
business_rules.then: The invocation MUST time out and return a failure result with the error details, preventing indefinite hangs
business_rules.business_impact: Without timeout limits, unresponsive applications would cause the evaluation UI to hang indefinitely, preventing operators from completing their work.

business_rules.given: A policy build workflow is already in progress
business_rules.when: An operator attempts to start a new build
business_rules.then: The system MUST resume polling the existing workflow instead of failing or creating a duplicate, handling the ResourceInUseException gracefully
business_rules.business_impact: Without resume capability, interrupted builds would require manual intervention and could leave the policy in an inconsistent state.

business_rules.given: An AR policy build workflow is running
business_rules.when: The build status transitions to FAILED
business_rules.then: The system MUST immediately terminate with the failure reason and MUST NOT proceed to the review or guardrail attachment steps
business_rules.business_impact: Continuing the pipeline after a failed build would attempt to use an incomplete or corrupted policy, leading to unreliable compliance checking.

business_rules.given: The AR policy build needs to ingest an ethics document
business_rules.when: Multiple document formats are available
business_rules.then: TXT format MUST be preferred over PDF format; if neither exists, the build MUST fail with an error
business_rules.business_impact: TXT format typically provides cleaner text extraction for rule parsing; using PDF when TXT is available may result in lower-quality rule extraction.

business_rules.given: The AR guardrail returns a translationAmbiguous finding
business_rules.when: The compliance system processes the finding
business_rules.then: All contradicting rules from ambiguous translations MUST be added to the failed rules list with an [ambiguous] prefix, treating ambiguity as a potential violation for safety
business_rules.business_impact: Ignoring ambiguous findings could allow potentially non-compliant manuscripts to pass review; treating them as failures ensures conservative compliance enforcement.

business_rules.rule_name: Audit Trail for Test Case Changes
business_rules.given: A test case is being created or updated
business_rules.when: The creation or update operation is executed
business_rules.then: The system MUST record the creator/updater identity, timestamp, change reason, and version — creating a complete audit trail for every modification
business_rules.business_impact: Without audit trails, it would be impossible to understand who changed test cases and why, undermining accountability and making it difficult to investigate evaluation anomalies.

business_flows.description: End-to-end setup of the entire GenAI Evaluation Framework from scratch, including infrastructure provisioning, application deployment, registration, prompt seeding, and test suite upload. This is the first-time setup flow that establishes all necessary resources.
steps: Step 1: Create infrastructure — S3 bucket (versioned) for golden datasets, DynamoDB tables for app registry, test case metadata, and prompt registry
Step 2: Deploy RAG application — create IAM role, upload FAQ documents to S3, package and deploy Lambda function, create API Gateway endpoint
Step 3: Register the RAG app in the app registry with type, endpoint, model provider, and ownership metadata
Step 4: Seed the default prompt version (v1) — auto-validate and activate it so the app can immediately serve responses
Step 5: Upload the golden test suite — bulk load curated test cases to both DynamoDB and S3

business_flows.description: The disciplined lifecycle for making changes to AI application behavior. New prompt configurations must go through creation, evaluation, validation, and explicit activation before they can serve production traffic. This prevents untested AI behavior from reaching users.
steps: Step 1: Create a new prompt version with system prompt, context template, temperature, max tokens, and model ID — version is created as inactive and unvalidated
Step 2: Test the prompt version against the golden test suite using prompt override (no activation needed) — invoke the app with _prompt_override to get responses
Step 3: Run evaluation — LLM-as-judge scores the responses, guardrails check for safety and grounding, results are aggregated
Step 4: If evaluation passes, mark the version as validated with the evaluation score
Step 5: Activate the validated version — deactivate the previous active version, update the active pointer, making the new prompt live in production

business_flows.description: End-to-end flow for answering a user question using the RAG application. The system loads the active prompt configuration, retrieves relevant documents, generates an AI response, and returns it with full traceability metadata.
steps: Step 1: Receive and validate the query — reject empty queries with HTTP 400
Step 2: Determine prompt configuration — use prompt override if provided (for eval testing), otherwise load the active prompt version from the registry (with fallback to defaults)
Step 3: Load FAQ documents from S3
Step 4: Retrieve top-K relevant documents using keyword matching (minimum relevance > 0)
Step 5: Generate answer — format the retrieved context using the prompt template, invoke the AI model with the configured system prompt, temperature, and token limits
Step 6: Return the answer along with metadata: retrieved document IDs, document count, and prompt version used

business_flows.description: Automated quality and safety evaluation of an AI application against a golden test suite. Combines LLM-as-judge scoring with guardrail safety checks to produce a comprehensive quality assessment.
steps: Step 1: Load the golden test suite (from snapshot for reproducibility, or from S3 for ad-hoc testing)
Step 2: For each test case, invoke the application with the test query (optionally with prompt override for candidate versions)
Step 3: Run LLM-as-judge — score the response against expected output across multiple dimensions (1-5 scale) using temperature 0.0 for deterministic results
Step 4: Run guardrail checks — verify content safety, PII handling, and contextual grounding (faithfulness to retrieved context)
Step 5: Aggregate results — combine judge scores and guardrail pass/fail across all test cases to determine overall evaluation outcome

business_flows.description: Lifecycle management of curated test cases used for evaluation. Supports creating, updating, and archiving test cases with full audit trails, plus snapshotting for reproducible evaluation runs.
steps: Step 1: Add test cases — specify query, expected output, ground truth context, evaluation criteria, category, criticality, and tags
Step 2: Manage test cases — update fields as needed (with audit trail), archive obsolete cases (never delete)
Step 3: Query test cases — filter by status (active/archived), criticality, and category
Step 4: Snapshot the active suite — create an immutable point-in-time copy in S3 for evaluation runs
Step 5: List and load snapshots — browse historical snapshots and load specific ones for evaluation

business_flows.description: Four-step pipeline to create, build, review, and activate an Automated Reasoning policy for publishing ethics compliance. Converts a natural-language ethics document into formal machine-verifiable rules.
steps: Step 1: Create the AR policy — register a named policy in Bedrock for Elsevier publishing ethics validation

Step 2: Build the policy — upload the ethics document (TXT preferred over PDF), start the rule extraction workflow, poll until extraction completes, and report the number of extracted variables, rules, and types

Step 3: Review the policy — inspect the fidelity report (coverage and accuracy scores), examine extracted variables, types, and rules, and check per-rule fidelity details. Warn if scores are below 0.7
Step 4: Attach to guardrail — create a Bedrock guardrail configured with the AR policy for runtime compliance enforcement, save the guardrail ID for use by compliance checking components

business_flows.description: End-to-end flow for verifying a manuscript submission's compliance with Elsevier's publishing ethics policies. Combines LLM-powered assessment generation with formal Automated Reasoning verification.
steps: Step 1: Receive a manuscript submission description

Step 2: Generate a structured compliance assessment using Claude AI, covering all 11 ethics dimensions (simultaneous submission, authorship, conflicts, funding, clinical trials, plagiarism, image manipulation)
Step 3: Submit the LLM assessment to the AR guardrail for formal verification against extracted policy rules
Step 4: Parse the AR response — categorize findings as invalid (rule violations with reasons), valid (satisfied rules), ambiguous (treated as potential violations), or unresolvable
Step 5: Determine final verdict — FAIL if any rules violated, UNABLE_TO_DETERMINE if only unresolvable, PASS if all rules satisfied
Step 6: Return the verdict with detailed per-rule results for compliance officer review

business_flows.description: Automated demonstration run that exercises all compliance scenarios against the AR system and produces a summary scorecard, used for validation and stakeholder demonstration of the compliance system's capabilities.
steps: Step 1: Load all predefined compliance scenarios with their expected verdicts
Step 2: For each scenario, generate an LLM compliance assessment from the scenario description
Step 3: Run the AR compliance check against the generated assessment
Step 4: Compare actual verdict against expected — FAIL and UNABLE_TO_DETERMINE both count as matches for expected FAIL scenarios
Step 5: Produce a summary table showing scenario name, expected verdict, actual verdict, and match status
Step 6: Report overall pass rate (N/M scenarios matched expected verdict)
